channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump Jr.: Biden administration trying to cause ‘World War 3’ by helping Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of President-elect Trump, claimed the Biden administration is trying to cause “World War 3” by helping Ukraine in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Moscow warns the US over allowing Ukraine to hit Russian soil with longer-range weapons
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Kremlin warned Monday that President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russia with U.S.-supplied longer-range missiles adds “fuel to the fire” of the war and would escalate international tensions even higher.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia: US adding 'fuel to the fire' with Ukraine missiles decision
item.description: The Kremlin on Monday issued an angry response to a reported U.S. decision to allow Ukraine to use Western-provided missiles to strike deep into Russian territory, accusing Washington of adding “fuel to the fire” in a war Moscow launched almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Teenage Buddhist lama celebrates last birthday in the U.S. before joining monastery in the Himalayas
item.description: ISANTI, Minn. (AP) — The young Buddhist lama sat on a throne near an altar decorated with flowers, fruits and golden statues of the Buddha, watching the celebrations of his 18th birthday in silence, with a faint smile.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starmer calls for strong UK-China relationship as he meets with Xi in Brazil
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he wants a strong and durable relationship with China as he met with Chinese President Xi Jinping at the G20 summit in Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Protesters in Georgia's capital set up a tent camp on the main street and call for new elections
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Demonstrators in the capital of Georgia have set up tents on one of the city's main thoroughfares and vowed Monday to stay there around the clock to demand new parliamentary elections in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Children and parents among 8 killed in Israeli strikes in Gaza, officials say
item.description: Palestinian officials say Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip killed eight people, including two children aged 7 and 9 and their parents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some Arab Americans who voted for Trump are concerned about his picks for key positions
item.description: Some Arab Americans are expressing concern over Donald Trump's picks for key positions in his next administration.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists face sentencing in Hong Kong. Here's who some of them are
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Dozens of prominent Hong Kong pro-democracy activists are scheduled to be sentenced Tuesday in the largest case under a national security law that critics say crushed political activism in the semi-autonomous Chinese city.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden has authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside Russia: Reports
item.description: President Biden authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike further into Russia, multiple reports said. The move comes as about 10,000 North Korean troops aid Russia in its war and expect to fight in Ukraine in the “next several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: British prime minister says he has no plans to talk with Putin as he reaffirms support for Ukraine
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he has no plan to speak with Russian President Vladimir Putin as he pledged support for Ukraine as the U.K.’s top priority at this week’s G20 summit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia has launched 120 missiles and 90 drones in a large-scale attack across Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Putin critics lead a march in Berlin calling for democracy in Russia and an end to war in Ukraine
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Prominent Russian opposition figures led a march of at least 1,000 people in central Berlin Sunday, criticizing Russian President Vladimir Putin and his war in Ukraine and calling for democracy in Russia. Behind a banner that read “No Putin.
item.dc:creator: MARKUS SCHREIBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: As U.S. President Joe Biden and China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time, Xi cautioned that a stable relationship between the two countries was critical to the 'future and destiny of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10 newborn babies die in India after fire rips through hospital neonatal unit
item.description: Ten newborn babies were killed in northern India after a fire swept through a neonatal intensive care unit on Friday; 38 newborns were reportedly rescued from the ward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump looms large as Biden set to meet China's Xi during Latin America summits
item.description: President Biden is in Latin America for the APEC and G-20 summits, in what is likely his last trip as president. The back-to-back summits come as an impending second Trump presidency is near, which may rattle some world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Iran told Biden administration it won't try to assassinate President-elect Trump: report
item.description: Iran reportedly gave the White House its promise that it will not attempt to kill Donald Trump just weeks before the Justice Department charged a murder-for-hire man in a scheme to kill the former president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Priest stabbed in the face during Mass as religion-based hate crime is on the rise worldwide
item.description: A priest stabbed in the face during Mass is one of the most recent cases of a religion-based hate crime, which is one the rise around the globe, though Jews are most often victims of the attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump said to lift all military restrictions on Israel on 1st day in office according to reports
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s promise could lead to a 60-day cease-fire, allowing Israel to suspend hostilities until military support resumes under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India's capital introduces stricter anti-pollution measures as toxic smog hides Taj Mahal
item.description: To combat worsening air quality, India's government has banned non-essential construction and encouraged residents to avoid burning coal for heating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US says it will not limit Israel arms transfers after some improvements in flow of aid to Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that Israel has made some good but limited progress in increasing the flow of humanitarian aid to Gaza and will not limit arms transfers to Israel as it had threatened to a month ago if the situation had not improved.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: President Joe Biden meets with Israel's Isaac Herzog
item.description: Joe Biden met with Israel President Isaac Herzog at the White House on Tuesday, which included a gift from Herzog to Biden.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
